metadata.name: ww-checkbox
metadata.description: A visual checkbox icon component that displays a customizable check mark. Must be used within ww-input-checkbox for proper functionality. It automatically receives checked and readonly states from its parent component.
features: Customizable icon color with state-based styling
Automatic state synchronization with parent ww-input-checkbox
Opacity-based visibility toggle (visible when checked, hidden when unchecked)
Placeholder icon display in editor mode
Does not handle click or toggle logic - must be placed inside ww-input-checkbox
Does not support standalone usage without a parent checkbox component

properties.name: checkboxColor
properties.description: The color of the checkbox icon only (not the background). Use transparent (#00000000) for unchecked state and a visible color for checked state via state-based props.

properties.name: icon
properties.description: The icon to display as the check mark. Uses the format 'library/icon-name' (e.g., 'lucide/check').

states.name: checked
states.description: Active when the checkbox is checked. Automatically synced with the parent ww-input-checkbox component's checked state.

states.name: readonly
states.description: Active when the checkbox is in readonly mode. Automatically synced with the parent ww-input-checkbox component's readonly state.

specifications: Must be used within a ww-input-checkbox component for proper checkbox functionality.
Size is controlled by setting width and height styles on the component, not fontSize.
Icon visibility is controlled automatically using opacity (0 when unchecked, 1 when checked).
The color property ONLY affects the icon/checkmark color, NOT the background.
ALWAYS include the 'checked' state in the states array for state-based styling.
Default icon color should be transparent (#00000000) to hide the checkmark when unchecked.
Checked state icon color should contrast with the background (e.g., white #FFFFFF on a colored background).
Background styling (backgroundColor, border) should be applied via styles, NOT the color prop.
DO NOT set icon color the same as background color - the icon needs to be visible when checked.
Styles must use named CSS properties (e.g., backgroundColor, borderRadius). Never use numeric indices or array-like keys in style objects.
